text.title: llm-powered-conversational-ai Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for llm-powered-conversational-ai as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: System prompt with "stay on topic" as the only topic boundary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs interpret "topic" as the conversational direction — they will follow user topic changes because that is "on topic" in a conversation. Explicit allowed/forbidden lists are the correct mechanism.

failure-mode.description: Testing persona consistency with only 3-5 turn demo scenarios.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM persona consistency degrades with conversation length. Test at 20+ turns and include adversarial turns. Demos that pass at 5 turns fail in production at 25 turns.

failure-mode.description: Assuming the LLM can detect emotion reliably for escalation triggers across all languages and dialects.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Emotion detection varies significantly with language, dialect, and cultural context. For critical escalations (angry customer), use explicit keywords from the user or conversation count thresholds, not emotion classification.

failure-mode.description: Trusting ASR output without validation on critical words.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 3% ASR word error rate on a critical word (product name, amount, action) can completely misinterpret the user intent. Route high-stakes turns through explicit confirmation dialogs.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated system prompts with 2000+ tokens due to verbose explanation and example inclusion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Token bloat increases latency and cost in multi-turn conversations. System prompt should be under 500 tokens. Move examples to few-shot turns, not system prompt body.

failure-mode.description: Agent designs conversation flows for only the happy path.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly prompt for error recovery, ambiguity handling, and out-of-scope scenarios. Happy-path-only designs fail on the first real user interaction that deviates from the expected flow.

failure-mode.description: Agent designs tool calls without accounting for network round-trip latency per call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each function/tool call in an LLM-powered voice system adds a full network round-trip. For real-time voice, batch tool calls or pre-fetch data to avoid visible pauses.

failure-mode.description: Agent runs identical system prompt without regression tests after any modification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: System prompt changes can break existing dialogue flows non-obviously. Run automated regression tests (Botium) on the full dialogue script corpus after every system prompt change.
